From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Slovenian Roman Catholic priest

| Christian Gostečnik | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1955-05-01)1 May 1955  [Šmihel nad Mozirjem](/wiki/%C5%A0mihel_nad_Mozirjem "Šmihel nad Mozirjem"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") |
| Alma mater | [University of Ljubljana](/wiki/University_of_Ljubljana "University of Ljubljana") [Loyola University Chicago](/wiki/Loyola_University_Chicago "Loyola University Chicago") [Argosy University](/wiki/Argosy_University "Argosy University") |
| Known for | Relational family therapy |
| **Scientific career** | |
| Fields | [Psychologist](/wiki/Psychologist "Psychologist") |
| Institutions | [Faculty of Theology, Ljubljana](/w/index.php?title=Faculty_of_Theology,_Ljubljana&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faculty of Theology, Ljubljana (page does not exist)") [Franciscan Family Institute](/w/index.php?title=Franciscan_Family_Institute&action=edit&redlink=1 "Franciscan Family Institute (page does not exist)") |
|  | |

**Christian Gostečnik**, [OFM](/wiki/Ordo_Fratrum_Minorum "Ordo Fratrum Minorum") (born 1 May 1955) is a Slovenian [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") [priest](/wiki/Priest "Priest"). He is a member of the [Franciscan](/wiki/Franciscan "Franciscan") [Order of Friars Minor](/wiki/Order_of_Friars_Minor "Order of Friars Minor"), and a theologian, clinical psychologist, and marriage-and-family therapist.

He was [dean](/wiki/Dean_(education) "Dean (education)") of the [Faculty of Theology](/w/index.php?title=Faculty_of_Theology,_Ljubljana&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faculty of Theology, Ljubljana (page does not exist)") at the [University of Ljubljana](/wiki/University_of_Ljubljana "University of Ljubljana"). He is a director of [Franciscan Family Institute](/w/index.php?title=Franciscan_Family_Institute&action=edit&redlink=1 "Franciscan Family Institute (page does not exist)") in [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana") ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Frančiškanski družinski inštitut*; *FDI*).

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Christian_Goste%C4%8Dnik&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Gostečnik was born in [Šmihel nad Mozirjem](/wiki/%C5%A0mihel_nad_Mozirjem "Šmihel nad Mozirjem"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). After he entered the Franciscan order, he started studying at the Faculty of Theology in Ljubljana, where he graduated in 1983.[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)

He was then sent to [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") for further studies and also as a member of Slovenian-American Parish in [Lemont, Illinois](/wiki/Lemont,_Illinois "Lemont, Illinois").[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1) During his stay in US he completed [M.A.](/wiki/Master_of_Arts "Master of Arts") on [Loyola University Chicago](/wiki/Loyola_University_Chicago "Loyola University Chicago") (1988), while at [Illinois School of Professional Psychology](/w/index.php?title=Illinois_School_of_Professional_Psychology&action=edit&redlink=1 "Illinois School of Professional Psychology (page does not exist)") ([Argosy University](/wiki/Argosy_University "Argosy University")) he completed specialization in Diagnostics (1991), specialization in marriage and family therapy (1993), internship in marriage and family therapy (1994) and PhD in clinical psychology (1995).[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)[[2]](#cite_note-TEOF-2) Gostečnik was also instrumental in establishment of [Slovenian Catholic Mission](http://www.lemont-svs.org) in Lemont in 1994.[[3]](#cite_note-Lemont-3)

In 1995 he returned to Ljubljana, Slovenia[[2]](#cite_note-TEOF-2) and became assistant professor (religious-educational psychology and medicine) at Faculty of Theology; in the same year he also established **Franciscan Family Center** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Frančiškanski družinski center*) (later renamed to current **Franciscan Family Institute**).[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)

In two years, he finished his postdoctoral study and received [doctorate of theology](/wiki/Doctorate_of_theology "Doctorate of theology").[[2]](#cite_note-TEOF-2) He then completed his third PhD study, this time from psychology at [Faculty of Arts, Ljubljana](/w/index.php?title=Faculty_of_Arts,_Ljubljana&action=edit&redlink=1 "Faculty of Arts, Ljubljana (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)[[2]](#cite_note-TEOF-2) In 2004 he was elected to associate professor and in 2009 to full professor.[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)[[4]](#cite_note-UniLJ-4)

During his academic career he "developed an innovative approach to marriage and family therapy called: [Relational family therapy](/w/index.php?title=Relational_family_therapy&action=edit&redlink=1 "Relational family therapy (page does not exist)")".[[2]](#cite_note-TEOF-2) In 2007 he was co-recipient of award for work on social services from Ministry of Work, Family and Social Affairs.[[1]](#cite_note-MDDSZ-1)

Between 2012 and 2016 he was dean of his [alma mater](/wiki/Alma_mater "Alma mater"): Faculty of Theology in Ljubljana.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
